rows.notes: Clear centered year at top of page, legible and unambiguous.

rows.description: Domus Fund Payments on acct of
rows.notes: Clear title line under the year. Slight uncertainty in final abbreviation 'acct' but text is legible.
Large clear brought-forward amount in the rightmost columns (1235.17.7). Vertical ruling lines are unambiguous and digits read clearly.
Both names legible. Amount 1/18/6 aligns clearly in the pounds/shillings/pence columns and digits are distinct.
Names reasonably legible; amount appears to be 1/8/6 but the shillings digit is slightly fainter and could be 18; chosen 1/8/6 based on spacing alignment with the small pounds column.
Description 'Damage to hedges' clearly readable. Amount 1/4/6 is well aligned and digits are crisp.
Surname legible. Amount reads as 2/1/6 though the '2' in pounds and the '1' in shillings are slightly less dark than surrounding lines.
Two names readable. Pounds column shows '1'. Shillings look like '19' and pence '10'; shillings two-digit reading is slightly cramped but within valid range.
Left name 'Braine' and center name 'Woolgrove' readable. Pounds read as 14 though the '1' is small; shillings and pence legible. Slight uncertainty on the tens digit alignment for pounds.
Names legible. In the pence column the digit is faint or possibly omitted; I recorded pounds 5 and shillings 7 and left pence blank because column shows no clear pence digit.
Center description 'Papering house &' visible. Amount reads 10/0/2 clearly; pence '2' is distinct but small.
Left name looks like 'Kedlington'. The pounds column is blank; shillings/pence show 6/6. Slight smearing around the shilling digit reduces confidence.
The center note 'Ranks Bill' (or 'Rands Bill') present. Pounds '15' clear; pence column is blank or not present, shillings appears 8 though size is light.
Name and centre note 'Thatching' readable. Amount 2/7/8 aligns with columns and digits are reasonably clear.
Surname repeated; centre 'Gates'. Pounds read as '1', shillings '11' but pence is either small or not present — left blank. Ink slightly faded here leading to moderate uncertainty.
Names legible. Amount looks like 1/18/3; the '18' shillings is close but digits readable. Moderate confidence due to slightly light ink on the tens digit.
Names legible. Pounds '3' and shillings '10' visible; pence column appears blank or illegible, left empty. Slight ink fade on pence area.
Left name Gardner appears again; centre looks like 'Stevves' (or 'Stevens'). Pounds column blank; amount shows 19/2 in s/d columns though the pounds-space may be omitted — alignment ambiguous.
Names legible. Pounds '1' clear. Shillings '2' visible but pence blank or too faint to read.
Centre 'Taylor' readable. Only a single shilling digit '7' visible in the shilling column; pounds and pence appear blank or not used. The isolated shilling is faint.

Central entry 'Inn Bile' (likely 'Inn Bill') under 'Gwinear' is legible as a header-like item. No monetary amount visible on this specific line; likely followed by detailed commission lines which carry amounts.

There is a small annotation 'Comm. £4.8' (commission £4.8) handwritten beneath 'Inn Bile' but it does not align with pounds/shillings/pence columns as a finished amount on this line. Handwritten currency notation uncertain; left amounts blank because those figures are summarized on the following subtotal line.

This line aggregates commission and stamps: the printed/right-aligned total reads 16/13/5 clearly. The centre text shows the breakdown '11.18.11' and '6.6' which sum to the shown total; alignment and digits are clear.
Single word 'Stamps' appears below the commission lines but no separate amount visible on this specific line. It seems to be part of the previous breakdown rather than a standalone amount.

Line reads 'Art'y manure' (artificial manure?) with a clear '10' in the shillings column; pounds and pence not shown. Slight ambiguity about whether '10' is shillings or pence due to spacing; interpreted as shillings based on alignment.
Name 'Kimber' and centre 'Poles' readable. Amount 1/6/6 aligns with the three vertical ruling lines and digits are distinct.
Centre word appears as 'Pepes' or 'Pipes'; amount shows 15/6 in s/d columns but no pounds. Shillings/pence slightly smudged; chosen 15/6 by best alignment.
The name and 'Thatching' are legible but the amount area is very faint or not present on this line; likely amount is on following line or omitted here.
Center text 'Art manure' readable. Amount not visible or omitted on this exact line; faint pen strokes prevent clear reading.
Names readable. Amount 1/8/6 is clearly aligned in the amount columns and digits are distinct.
Center 'Self. Straw' readable. Pounds appear as '9' and shillings '15' though the two-digit shilling is slightly cramped; pence appears blank.

Center 'White Thatching' legible. The pounds column shows '4' and shillings appear as '18'; pence column either blank or illegible. Two-digit shilling reading slightly uncertain but within 0-19 range.
Name appears as 'Clinckard' (or 'Clinckand'). Amount shows 2/9 with clear digits in pounds and shillings; pence not present.
Following line for same surname; centre reads 'Pepes' or 'Pipes'. Amount reads 6/2 in shillings/pence columns; pounds blank. Shillings digit a bit faint.
Name 'Churchill' and centre 'Comm'n' visible. Amount reads 2/2 in pounds/shillings; pence blank. Digits are reasonably clear.
Final boxed total at bottom-right reads £1350.4.11 clearly in the ruled box. Alignment and digits are crisp; classification as a total is evident from the box and phrasing.
